items.q: How do SSRIs help with OCD?
items.correct: They prevent reabsorption of serotonin, increasing its availability in the synapse.
items.misconception: They directly produce more serotonin in the brain.
items.why: Students think SSRIs create serotonin. They actually inhibit REUPTAKE - serotonin already released stays in the synapse longer, increasing post-synaptic stimulation.

items.q: What is the behaviourist view on the nature-nurture debate?
items.correct: Strongly nurture - we are shaped by environmental conditioning.
items.why: Behaviourists reject innate ideas (tabula rasa - blank slate). Students soften this to 'a mix'. Watson and Skinner explicitly attributed nearly all behaviour to environment.

items.q: What are the three elements of Beck's negative triad?
items.correct: Negative views of self, world, and future.
items.misconception: Negative views of past, present, and future.
items.why: Students often substitute 'past' for 'self' or 'world'. The triad is about the DOMAINS of negative thought (self/world/future), not time periods.

items.q: Miller (1956) estimated short-term memory capacity at:
items.why: Correct answer: 7 plus or minus 2. “3 plus or minus 1” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: The case of HM provided support for the multi-store model because he:
items.why: It's Lost LTM but kept STM. “Lost STM but kept LTM” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Approximately how many seconds is STM duration in Peterson and Peterson?
items.why: Exact answer: 18 seconds. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
